messages.docs.index.title: Documentation
messages.help.issues.title: About this section
messages.help.issues.body: Issues are events automatically grouped by similar errors: the same exception type and code location are merged into one issue with a stack trace, frequency, and occurrence history.
messages.help.performance.title: About this section
messages.help.performance.body: This page lists your application's transactions and endpoints with response-time percentiles (p50/p95/p99), error rate, and Apdex — useful for spotting the slowest and least stable spots.
messages.help.metrics.title: About this section
messages.help.metrics.body: Metrics are time series of arbitrary numeric application values (counters, histograms) that can be aggregated and viewed over a chosen time period.
messages.help.uptime.title: About this section
messages.help.uptime.body: Monitors periodically check the availability of your services and create incidents on failures — this page shows current status, incident history, and uptime percentage.
messages.help.alerts.title: About this section
messages.help.alerts.body: Alert rules define which events (new issue, regression, error spike) trigger notifications, and channels define where they are delivered (email and other integrations).
messages.help.alertdeliveries.title: About this section
messages.help.alertdeliveries.body: This page shows the most recent failed deliveries of notifications to channels (email, webhook, Telegram) — with the error text and the number of attempts.
messages.help.maintenance.title: About this section
messages.help.maintenance.body: Maintenance windows let you temporarily pause monitor checks so planned work doesn't create false incidents and notifications — one-off or weekly, by weekday and time.
messages.help.statuspages.title: About this section
messages.help.statuspages.body: A public status page shows visitors the current state of selected monitors without giving them access to the rest of the project — a convenient way to keep users informed during an incident.
messages.help.probes.title: About this section
messages.help.probes.body: Probes are agents in different regions that run monitor checks from that location — this lets you confirm a service is reachable from more than one place and tell a service outage apart from a region-specific network problem.
messages.help.metricalerts.title: About this section
messages.help.metricalerts.body: Metric alert rules watch a chosen metric (an aggregation compared against a threshold over a time window) and open an incident when the condition is met — this page also shows the history of past incidents.
messages.help.webvitals.title: About this section
messages.help.webvitals.body: Web Vitals are real-user page load experience metrics: LCP (main content render speed), INP (responsiveness to interaction), and CLS (visual stability). The good / needs-improvement / poor thresholds are Google's standard Web Vitals bounds, the same for everyone and not configurable per project.
messages.help.profiles.title: About this section
messages.help.profiles.body: Profiles show where CPU time goes inside your code: expand a group of profiles for the selected period into a flame graph to see the "heaviest" functions by self-CPU share.
messages.help.perfissues.title: About this section
messages.help.perfissues.body: Automatically detected performance problems: N+1 database queries, slow queries, and HTTP floods, each with the code location and how often it occurs.
messages.help.regressions.title: About this section
messages.help.regressions.body: Regressions are a sustained worsening of an endpoint's p95 duration or a web vital's p75 compared to a recent baseline: baseline, peak, and percent increase are shown so you can quickly gauge the severity.
messages.help.profileregressions.title: About this section
messages.help.profileregressions.body: Profile regressions track a function's self-CPU share growing over time — a way to spot code that used to be cheap becoming noticeably slower, before users complain.
messages.brand.tagline: Catch errors before your users do.

messages.err.probe.name_region: probe name and region must be non-empty and at most 40 characters

messages.err.metricalert.threshold_finite: threshold must be a finite number
messages.err.metricalert.window_positive: window must be a positive number of seconds
